# claude-managed-agents_agent (Data Source)

Look up an existing Claude Managed Agents agent by id.

## Example Usage

```terraform
# Look up an existing agent by id. Useful when the agent was created outside
# of Terraform (e.g. via the SDK) and you want to reference its current state.

data "claude-managed-agents_agent" "existing" {
  id = "agent_01HqR2k7vXbZ9mNpL3wYcT8f"
}

output "existing_agent_name" {
  value = data.claude-managed-agents_agent.existing.name
}

output "existing_agent_version" {
  value = data.claude-managed-agents_agent.existing.version
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `id` (String) Server-assigned identifier (e.g. `agent_01ABC...`).

### Read-Only

- `archived_at` (String) ISO 8601 archive timestamp, or null if active.
- `created_at` (String) ISO 8601 creation timestamp.
- `description` (String) Description, or null.
- `metadata` (Map of String) Metadata map.
- `model` (String) Model identifier.
- `name` (String) Agent name.
- `system` (String) System prompt, or null.
- `updated_at` (String) ISO 8601 last-modified timestamp.
- `version` (Number) Server-managed version number.
